title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard protocol that enables AI agents and applications to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a common architecture. MCP uses JSON-RPC for client-server communication and supports both local and remote connections via STDIO or HTTP transports.
writeup: This video explains the Model Context Protocol (MCP), a standard that lets AI agents and applications connect to external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a common architecture. It breaks down the MCP client, MCP server, and the overall architecture, including how the protocol uses JSON-RPC for client-server communication. The key takeaway is that MCP supports both local and remote connections through STDIO or HTTP transports, providing a uniform interface for agent orchestration across varied backends.
